author: tom-sherman

body:
> the context menu is a bit clunky. Could we not add lock under actions? next to link? at least on the mobile menu?

The placement of lock between duplicate and delete can be improved, I feel. Moving it above duplicate would be better, IMO. Maybe there are other alternative placements.
author: chipbite

body:
I'm not sure it's a good idea to put the lock in the actions menu, because for unlocking you must go through contextMenu anyway.

As such, keeping it symmetrical is better in terms of UX — if users don't already know that unlocking is done via contextMenu and they mistakenly lock an item from the sidebar (and deselect the element) as part of trying out what the button does, they won't know how to unlock.

But, I agree the contextMenu placement isn't great due to being too close to delete. I think we should add another separate between `Delete` and the other items.

author: dwelle

body:
I've changed the `excludeLockedElements` to `includeLockedElements` (inverted), IOW default to not selecting locked elements, which aligns it with the `includeBoundTextElement` arg, and fixes other cases as well.

Before we merge I want to do a proper review and refactor, as this is a larger change.

We should also write some tests for this, such as:

- [x] click-selecting or box-selecting locked elements is disabled
- [x] dragging locked elements is disabled
- [x] `selectAll` ignores locked elements
- [x] opening contextmenu on selected element that's covered by a locked element prefers the selected element
- [x] opening contextmenu on a locked element prefers the locked element even if there's an unlocked element below it
- [x] selecting a group containing locked element(s) selects all including the locked elements
- [x] locked text element is not considered in any of the cases around text-bindable containers (creating/editing bound text)

body:
Cool! It would have probably taken me some time to figure this out 😄.

```
pointerDownState.hit.element.link!.length > 0
```

The check above throws when link is empty — it'd have to be `pointerDownState.hit.element.link?.length` for it to work. But instead, I moved the check inside `isPointHittingLinkIcon`. I'm also checking whether the element is selected, because when it is, we don't render the link icon so the check should return false.

Plus some unrelated refactor.

body:
> lint Expected — Waiting for status to be reported
> test Expected — Waiting for status to be reported

Force pushing the change to get lint and test checks reported, is there a known issue?
author: valgussev

body:
Thanks. I've taken a look and decided to do fix this differently by resetting the state in the finalize action — this will fix even more cases. Also doing the same on `restore()`.

> Force pushing the change to get lint and test checks reported, is there a known issue?

It just requires a maintainer approval to run GH actions when a first-time collaborator creates a PR.

author: vercel

body:
Actually, this isn't working quite right.

1. You should be able to copy text of selected elements even if there are other non-text elements on the scene
2. It should only copy selected elements, not all elements on canvas
3. The contextMenu action should only work on selection (elements), not on canvas

Haven't reviewed the code itself.
author: dwelle

body: I'd also put `\n\n` between texts of each element, i.e. make each element into a paragraph. Though this is debatable, I think it makes sense because each text element usually is its own thought, so bunching everything together isn't as useful, especially considering that you wouldn't be able to distinguish between single element line breaks and multiple elements.
author: dwelle

body:
> Actually, this isn't working quite right.
> 
> 1. You should be able to copy text of selected elements even if there are other non-text elements on the scene
> 2. It should only copy selected elements, not all elements on canvas
> 3. The contextMenu action should only work on selection (elements), not on canvas
> 
> Haven't reviewed the code itself.

Oh yea, you're right. I'll make a follow up PR

author: jasonphillips

body:
hey @jasonphillips — sorry for the delay, too busy lately.

One note though: we generally only refer to the market share when it comes to deciding whether to include and/or transpile an API, so there may still be APIs we use that are >=ES2018 (it's hard to orient oneself what feature was introduced which year since MDN/caniuse don't list it).

Merging!

author: vercel

body:
> I hope "unstable" here concerns the API, and is more or less safe 😛

Yeah, what it means is the api is not fixed (like `@excalidraw/excalidraw-next`) and it's not yet widely used (but it's just a tiny wrapper implementation-wise).
author: dai-shi

body:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5046#discussion_r858997454

That reminds me. @dwelle You want to use `scope` for all `useAtom` hooks and the `Provider` component. This avoids conflicting stores when excalidraw package users happen to use jotai in their apps.
author: dai-shi

body:
> That reminds me. @dwelle You want to use `scope` for all `useAtom` hooks and the `Provider` component. This avoids conflicting stores when excalidraw package users happen to use jotai in their apps.

Yes! This PR's jotai implem was actually superseded by this PR https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5067, I just have to rebase this one on master.

author: vercel

body:
> I agree we should save only non deleted elements to firebase. @dwelle do you see any concern regarding this?

It will cause a small (hopefully small?) race condition on reconciliation from firebase (after the collab rewrite is merged. Actually, even with the current algo, albeit it'd be a different race condition). So I'd only drop deleted elements older than X. We can make it older than a day to be safe.
author: dwelle

body:
> It will cause a small (hopefully small?) race condition on reconciliation from firebase (after the collab rewrite is merged. Actually, even with the current algo, albeit it'd be a different race condition). So I'd only drop deleted elements older than X. We can make it older than a day to be safe.

@dwelle @ad1992 Done in 3337ab3.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
I moved the filter logic out from `encryptElements()` to `getSyncableElements()`. Perhaps we want to make a distinction between syncing (collab) and persisting (database), but right now I don't think there should be.

I've also made sure that we filter out the elements on fetch, and that we cache the version correctly accounting for syncable elements.

author: vercel

body:
@ryan-di finally managed to find some time. Thanks for starting this off!

Wrote tests where I found some edge cases your algo didn't catch. So I went and tried slightly different way to handle this which started off much simpler, but turned more complex as I fixed some cases.

The new algo should be faster, and maybe easier to reason about. It works like this: loop through unsorted array. If the element is a container, find bound text element and push after the container, and replace with `null` in the orig array so we know we should skip it later. If the element is a text element and it has a container who lists it as a bound element, then skip it as it'll be taken care of once we get to it. But if the container doesn't exist or doesn't list it as a bound element (= invalid state), ensure the child element isn't thrown away.

I've also added a group order normalization. If it arrives at an element that's part of a group, we find all group elements, and then we recursively order subgroups within that group.

Right now I'm first re-ordering the groups and re-ordering the bound elements. This means the groups can get mis-ordered again during the 2nd step, so ultimately we should ensure the state gets fixed (probably by unbinding the elements if one of the elements isn't in the group it should be in), but I left it for now.

Also, since I'm not super confident I caught all the edge cases, I've added a check to ensure we're not dropping or adding more elements by checking the orig vs new length (and using a `Set` to catch duplicates), because worse than messing up the state is losing people's elements.

Lemme know if you find something, else we can merge!
author: dwelle

body: @dwelle 🤩 Appreciate the amount of care that you've put into this fix! Looks good to me!
author: ryan-di

body:
I rewrote the duplication loop part a bit so we don't rely on indexes as it's possible we backtrack (or clone previously processed elements) when handling groups (especially around deleted elements https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5767) and in the future possibly other things. See [`7de6840` (#5130)](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5130/commits/7de684075f6eef5c42535384c1e1c95fa2f1b79d).

What I'm doing is two passes. In the first I clone elements, not caring too much about duplicates. In the second pass, looping from the end, I remove any duplicates. The cost of double loop and some of the other things I do is not much relevant in the context of element duplication, so I didn't bother to optimize much.

body: What should happen if user imports a file that contains images?
author: dwelle

body:
Good question. The major use case for this option is when I don't want to provide binary image storage for my users' Excalidraw drawings, so it wouldn't make sense to let them add images via import since they'd just break on page reload.

I suppose it should remove the images from the file at import time, any maybe display a warning? What's your opinion?
author: cynicaloptimist

body:
thanks @cynicaloptimist for this PR ... i have the exact same need (i'm stuck in 0.10 for that... and start to experience issue with it).

IMHO uploading a file with image should ignore those.... as you stated we don't want the images in the end results
author: eMerzh

body: @dwelle I handled the paste and drag cases as you mentioned on Discord. Not sure about the best approach for removing images from an imported drawing. I could add an optional parameter to `restore()` from `restore.ts` to filter out image elements there, for instance. Other ideas?
author: cynicaloptimist

body:
Hi @cynicaloptimist. I'm really sorry for dropping this one — to the extent that I forgot it exists and on one evening made almost a duplicate PR https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6320/files 😭.

In my PR I'm handling a bit more cases. And we also need to handle the importing case as you mention above.

If you still want to work on this, then I'd suggest you pull some of the changes I'm doing in my PR (mainly the app.tsx drag&drop and blob related things including error handling) into this one.

But it's fine if you want to move on, in which case I'll likely close this PR and wrap it up in my own.

Let me know your thoughts!
author: dwelle

body: Thanks for the response- I don't remember enough context to pick this back up so feel free to close the PR. 👍

body:
thanks @Periclod — you're right, I should be reading my own comments! This is a problem though, since the mutation caused a different race condition: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5143/files. Will need to re-check what exactly it was causing and how can we fix both bugs at the same time.

That said, I'd rather solve the current bug, because depending on mutation is brittle as later we might be migrating to a solution where we'll never be mutating.
author: dwelle

body:
I think you pasted the wrong link :D Or what race condition do you mean?

What's the solution you're planning to migrate to?

Without mutation `state.draggingElement` & other references to members of syncElements should be ids
They'll have to be fetched whenever they are read and replaced in the array when updated

Happens to me often. Here it is: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4147 — the PR where I changed it to non-mutation to fix a different issue. I'll have to reproduce it coz I don't remember what it was exactly (hard to write tests for these things) and decide what next.

body:
A bug I just found, hopefully will be solved when I implement expanding/contracting ranges but noting it down to check later:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9257001/167318197-550480d1-9056-45d4-a600-b367cd5b5ee6.mov

Basically the stroke/caret color doesn't update correctly when removing chars from the end of a color range.
author: tom-sherman

body:
@DanielJGeiger @dwelle Could I get a sanity check on the approach here? Especially with regards to how we're storing `colorRanges` on text.

Just want to make sure this is on the right track before I go deep into implementing color range change on insertion/deletion/replacement of text.
author: tom-sherman

body: I found a slight bug: when editing text, I have to click on the "#" symbol before clicking the stroke color, or else the entire text reverts to the default color. This issue only occurs after committing the edits made (like by pressing the "Esc" key).

body:
I am glad this is moving forward.  For further extensions of #5164, here are some considerations from #2993 besides my [earlier](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4963#issuecomment-1100197609) [comments](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4963#issuecomment-1113890941):

`Text` elements possess various properties unique to text.  Custom `Text` elements may use some or all of these, while adding more of their own.  For example, `Math` elements in #2993 don't need multiple font families.  But `Math` elements support standard/simplified input (Latex/AsciiMath), besides mixed-text/math-only display modes.

Custom `Text` elements would ideally support standard `Text` features like #5150, #3890, etc.  I propose some "`Text` element" API so custom `Text` elements can extend the WYSIWYG principle (eg a mixed-text mode `Math` element which contains no math input).  See #3915 for an initial attempt at such an API.

The element properties sidebar forms a standard part of the Excalidraw UI.  This would provide a natural way for users to set custom elements' properties (`Text` or otherwise).  #2993 demonstrates the UX from #3915's attempt at this, including i18n aspects.

Charts feature: It could be useful for the titles and/or axis labels of charts to support custom `Text` elements (eg `Math`) by default.
author: DanielJGeiger

body: From playing around a little more, I actually think that if anything, me may actually want to reduce the zoom amount when very zoomed out.
author: johtso

body:
> From playing around a little more, I actually think that if anything, me may actually want to reduce the zoom amount when very zoomed out.

are we talking about trackapad or in general?
author: dwelle

body:
> are we talking about trackapad or in general?

Ok, playing with it, it's definitely wild. On macbook trackpad, it's high, but manageable — but we should still lower it across all levels of zoom, not just when zoomed out.

But mouse wheel is not not very usable after the change — previously zooming with mouse wheel was very solid, so I'd try to match the same.
author: dwelle

body:
After taking another look, I think the whole "normalization" thing was a bit of a red herring. The real issue was that we were rounding every zoom change to the nearest "zoom step", and because trackpads produce lots of tiny wheel events, they never hit the next zoom step until you are swiping way too fast.

Removing that rounding, things are a fair bit better.

The speed of zooming is still way too fast when over 100%, but I'm not sure how to improve that without affecting the mechanical scroll wheel behaviour which is okay at the moment. I don't know of any way to distinguish between a mechanical scroll wheel and a trackpad. Possibly we could do something as simple as changing behaviour if the delta is over a certain amount..
author: johtso

body: With the "reduced amplification for small deltas" tweak things are working quite nicely!
author: johtso

body:
### :warning:  socket.io-client
<details>
<summary> Breaking changes</summary>



<details>
<summary>1: Change in Event Emitted on Connection Error</summary>

> The Socket instance now emits a 'connect_error' event instead of 'error'. Update your code to listen for 'connect_error' instead of 'error'.
</details>


<details>
<summary>2: Removal of Socket#binary() Method</summary>

> The Socket#binary() method has been removed. If your code uses this method, replace it with your own parser.
</details>


<details>
<summary>3: Socket Instance No Longer Forwards Manager's Events</summary>

> The Socket instance no longer forwards the events of its Manager. If your code listens for these events on the Socket instance, update it to listen on the Manager instance.
</details>


</details>



> :bust_in_silhouette: Who am I? I am a bot that helps developers to update dependencies by informing if changelogs contain breaking changes or not.

> :wave: See you again? If you want my help with other dependency updates, [add me](https://github.com/apps/adaptly-bot) to your repository.

> PS. I hope I didn't bother you - my purpose is to help developers and I won't comment anymore without your request. Feel free to shoot me a message about anything support@adaptly.dev. Cheers.
author: adaptly-bot

body: Initial thoughts Lets make this responsive, rn the the sidebar gets cut off as shown above Also We should open it below the "Introducing Excalidraw+" so that top right UI (introducing Excal+) including the shapes toolbar doesn't shift left when the sidebar is opened as thats distracting
author: ad1992

body:
> Also We should open it below the "Introducing Excalidraw+" so that top right UI (introducing Excal+) including the shapes toolbar doesn't shift left when the sidebar is opened as thats distracting

The spec was actually to _shift_ the UI as @ishtiaqbhatti is doing, because we 1) want the sidebar to be full height (which currently in the PR it isn't? /cc @ishtiaqbhatti), and 2) so it doesn't cover other editor UI that could happen to be underneath the sidebar if we didn't shift the UI.

Also, we shouldn't close the sidebar when we drop an item on the canvas — that was one of the two main reasons for this redesign. We can later decide whether to support disabling this new behavior if users wish (as @ishtiaqbhatti suggested in chat).
author: dwelle

If the sidebar goes up to full height then its fine but still do we want to give it full height ? I think we can leave the space for the shapes and excal + link and start the sidebar below that so that there is no UI(shapes panel + top right excal+ link), shifting, rest all specs remain same

author: ad1992

body:
> The loading icon when importing appears on next line, I think we can remove it or show it in place of load icon ?

The purpose of the spinner is:

1) to give visual cue something is happening. Needed when installing from the network, but potentially also when installing from fs if the initializing takes a long time (once we support images in libraries, that will be the case).

2) prevent browsing/installing other libraries while processing. This is not as important for our app since there shouldn't be race conditions now, but for host apps there may be.

In previous design, the spinner showed in place of the `browse libraries` button. In the sidebar design, we'll likely need to 1) disable the `browse libraries` button (instead of hiding), and 2) show the spinner somewhere else. Not sure where though.
author: dwelle

body: @dwelle what do you think about showing spinner in place of load button instead? Since we are importing libs so showing in place of load button would be better.
author: ad1992

body:
> what do you think about showing spinner in place of load button instead? Since we are importing libs so showing in place of load button would be better.

what about when you're installing from the repository? IMO we should have just one spinner.

Speaking of which, I'm not sure whether we shouldn't revert the "browser libraries" button to be in the "original" place, below the top buttons (same as in the floating menu currently).

body:
On Windows, works in Chrome, but doesn't work in FF. I think it's because `resize` isn't triggered on zoom in FF. When I then resize the browser, the toast shows. Strangely enough, FF on MacOS works.

The resize event doesn't trigger on Android Chrome either, if you manage to zoom (there it's less likely you'll manage unless you have it enabled in accessibility settings).
author: dwelle

body: IMO the toast should be visible permanently (or unless dismissed with an X button).
author: dwelle

body:
> On Windows, works in Chrome, but doesn't work in FF. I think it's because resize isn't triggered on zoom in FF. When I then resize the browser, the toast shows. Strangely enough, FF on MacOS works.

Thats interesting coz in Mac it works fine

> IMO the toast should be visible permanently (or unless dismissed with an X button).

I am showing the toast but when its 100% I am hiding it else it can create confusion
author: ad1992

body:
> I am showing the toast but when its 100% I am hiding it else it can create confusion

Not sure what you mean. The toast currently autohides after a few seconds of being shown. This means that people may not notice it or not finish reading/understanding what it means before it's gone.
author: dwelle

body:
> > On Windows, works in Chrome, but doesn't work in FF. I think it's because resize isn't triggered on zoom in FF. When I then resize the browser, the toast shows. Strangely enough, FF on MacOS works.
> 
> Thats interesting coz in Mac it works fine
> 
> > IMO the toast should be visible permanently (or unless dismissed with an X button).
> 
> I am showing the toast but when its 100% I am hiding it else it can create confusion

Btw I just checked in a windows machine and its working fine in firefox
 I think it might be related to the logic due to which toast isn't shown. For the windows machine I was checking, `window.devicePixelRatio` was 3 at 100% due to which even at 100% the toast was being shown
author: ad1992

body:
> > I am showing the toast but when its 100% I am hiding it else it can create confusion
> 
> Not sure what you mean. The toast currently autohides after a few seconds of being shown. This means that people may not notice it or not finish reading/understanding what it means before it's gone.

Oops ok I misunderstood with hiding when 100%, sure I will prevent autohiding and make it dismissable
author: ad1992

body:
> Btw I just checked in a windows machine and its working fine in firefox

Weird. When I bind a `resize` listener, it doesn't fire in Firefox:

```
window.addEventListener('resize', () => console.log('resize'))
```
author: dwelle

body: @dwelle I have removed the `window.devicePixelRatio` check and updated the threshold values to detect zoom, can you check if it works fine for you ?

author: vercel

body:
HI @DanielJGeiger, sorry for not being responsive on your PR. 
Before we start the review I was thinking about a plugin like integration where users can install the extra add-ons / plugins for more functionalities.

* This would also mean that it will be completely decoupled from the codebase and will go into some repo lets say `@excalidraw/plugins` which is a big plus point
* It will not add it by default keeping Excalidraw as is and interested users can install the extension to try out
* It will be easier and faster to add extensions 

This will mean we will need to come up with a workflow / UI and API for plugins as well which would take some time but I think this this would be worth it and will make the process of adding plugins very smooth and easier once merged in the repo `@excalidraw/plugins`.

Let me know your thoughts. cc @dwelle.
author: ad1992

body:
Hi @ad1992, thanks for your reply.  I think `@excalidraw/plugins` is a great idea.

I would propose making a version of https://excalidraw.com with `@excalidraw/plugins` included, something like https://full.excalidraw.com.  Then academics like myself could have our students use https://full.excalidraw.com without pointing them to preview versions of PRs.  @mcaruel @baptiste what do you think?
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
Very much in favour of having a permanent URL like you suggest, to access optional plugin functionalities (I suggested "dev.excalidraw.com" at some point, I think). Maybe the list of plugin(s) in use could be user-selected, if some happen to be incompatible with others or just not needed.

You've done a fantastic job Daniel, with this Mathjax integration, and I imagine a lot of STEM instructors will jump at this opportunity to combine online sketches and maths for their virtual classes. The only thing that's prevented me from sharing it with academic colleagues for the past year is a lack of a permanent-looking URL (giving confidence that it's worth investing time in the tool).

Thanks so much for your continued efforts on this.
author: baptiste

body: @baptiste @jcezarms @ad1992 @dwelle User selection of plugins sounds good, with one catch.  E.g. I wouldn't want to make my students enable math support every time they connected to the semester whiteboard.  I suggest offering something like [full|bio|compsci|math].excalidraw.com with different common sets of plugins enabled.  This could avoid storing extra info in Firebase.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
@ad1992 @dwelle What do you think of the following?  Let's have an initial release of the `@excalidraw/plugins` API support `ExcalidrawElement` subtype plugins, with MathJax as an example.  This PR would land in both packages: `src/subtypes/math/*` would go in `@excalidraw/plugins`, and the rest in `@excalidraw/excalidraw` as part of the API and UI.

This would also benefit people who wanted to create plugins for Kroki/Mermaid etc.  Then future releases would expand the `@excalidraw/plugins` API.
author: DanielJGeiger

body: @dwelle @ad1992 I'm trying marking all my PRs as drafts except for the one I'd for you to review next.  Hopefully this makes it easier for you to review my work amid everything you have going on as the lead @excalidraw devs.
author: DanielJGeiger

body: @DanielJGeiger : You're doing such a great job. This LaTeX feature is really cool !
author: mcaruel

body: This definitely is a fine addition. I've tested it in multiple different settings and it always delivered. @ad1992 what work is left for this to be included into excalidraw, even if as a plugin? Even considering a new repo would have to be created for plugins, I reckon outlining the prerequisites of such work is important. This feature adds a very common need into whiteboard experience, as most scientific work will involve (even if differing) levels of math work too.
author: jcezarms
DanielJGeiger

body: @jcezarms @mcaruel @baptiste This PR is starting to become cluttered itself, with Vercel deployments of force-pushes.  Maybe further discussions should go in #1176 or #5265.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
For anyone interested, I have published a nightly container build of excalidraw <https://github.com/rwxd/excalidraw-container>.

The newest v0.12.0 excalidraw tag is also published on Docker Hub & GitHub.
author: rwxd

body:
Hey @rwxd thanks for the contribution!. 
The build is fixed now so can you rebase your branch with master ? and we can discuss on better tagging then.
author: ad1992

body:
Hi @ad1992 I rebased my branch.  

The tagging improvements would give users more opportunities to choose a version instead of latest or the commit hash.

I use the tags functionality on my [repository](https://github.com/rwxd/excalidraw-container). The created tags can be seen on [DockerHub](https://hub.docker.com/r/rwxd/excalidraw/tags).

author: vercel

body:
> This PR makes sure that text dimensions are recalculated on restore, except during collaboration for perf reasons (we should take another look into rewriting our current text dimensions calculation to use the [measureText](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CanvasRenderingContext2D/measureText)).
> 
>     * [x]  changelog/readme

Reading through the code changes, this looks good overall.  Perhaps rewriting text dimensions calculations would come after `@excalidraw/plugins` lands?  Text-based plugins like MathJax or UML should be able to calculate dimensions themselves.
author: DanielJGeiger

body: I've removed the `refreshDimensions` param from `restore()` to reduce the API added, and reduce breaking changes if (once) we remove it in the future. For full state restore, perf is less likely to matter.

body: This seems very useful.
author: 7flash

body: Any updates on this? This feature would rock!
author: sojusnik

body:
This looks awesome!
I want to use Excalidraw to take notes on PDFs (well I guess image printouts of pdfs) but at this stage it wouldnt be useful at all as I wouldn't be able to scroll through to the relevant time I wrote "state space modelling" (instead of the 800 other times in the same excalidraw doc)
author: sstar0016

body:
> This looks awesome! I want to use Excalidraw to take notes on PDFs (well I guess image printouts of pdfs) but at this stage it wouldnt be useful at all as I wouldn't be able to scroll through to the relevant time I wrote "state space modelling" (instead of the 800 other times in the same excalidraw doc)

Are you importing those as images? I have actually built a feature that makes text from them transcribed and saved into database, allowing to perform the search globally beyond the current canvas. Let me know if that's what you need I will open a PR.
author: 7flash

body:
> Are you importing those as images? I have actually built a feature that makes text from them transcribed and saved into database, allowing to perform the search globally beyond the current canvas. Let me know if that's what you need I will open a PR.

Yes! That would be absolutely incredible! 
My current workflow is me trying to use Excalidraw+Obsidian as a Onenote replacement:
- take pdf and use online converter to download zip file of page jpgs
- import into excali and rearrange using your (@7flash) excellent script [Grid Selected Images](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/blob/master/ea-scripts/Grid%20Selected%20Images.md )
- Take notes

My current big issues are
- search option kinda sucks in excali for my workflow (so your offer would be greatly appreciated)

Feature improvements that would be cool:
- Fix a bug that makes integration with Obsidian a bit rubbish [BUG: Issues with the dimensions of area transclusions](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/issues/967 )
- A script to automate the process of importing pdfs so I could just select the file and it would insert the file and do OCR
author: sstar0016

body: @sstar0016 Question about OCR: would you like to see the image actually replaced with text, either the text to be associated with the image element for searching purpose only? Thinking out loud, perhaps can keep both image because it looks better with original formatting and also text for copying on double click, like in iphones?
author: 7flash

body:
> perhaps can keep both image because it looks better with original formatting and also text for copying on double click, like in iphones?

Yeah 100%, that would be above and beyond what I would expect but if that is possible that would be incredible. 

The dream ocr feature would be text in location so if #5435 gets put into use we'd be able to have similar functionality to below, where we can go through all instances of a word in a doc, whether we wrote it or it is scanned in
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/124852454/218209481-2116a95a-292c-4851-8729-b67a93c1df09.png)




![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/124852454/218208824-edc2fbb3-62c8-47ce-96bb-59abfc452eb9.png)
author: sstar0016

body: @sstar0016 Do you mind if search results are not shown in UI but rather appear as text/link elements referencing to specific coordinates on the canvas?
author: 7flash

body:
> @sstar0016 Do you mind if search results are not shown in UI but rather appear as text/link elements referencing to specific coordinates on the canvas?

I would be super happy with any implementation of search, I can adjust to any implementation so long as the functionality is there. I do think that simple text coordinates would not be as helpful as a link that would bring you to the point precisely but again, I would be greatly appreciative of any implementation that gets closer to the functionality Onenote has
author: sstar0016

body: Would merging this make the `docs` preview build in #5483 and subsequent PRs?
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
> Would merging this make the `docs` preview build in #5483 and subsequent PRs?

yeah once they are rebased with master after this is merged. Any concern with that ?

EDIT ->  you are right this will start triggering for every PR's, I will add a check to make sure this triggers only for docs related changes
author: ad1992

body: Docs will only build when any change made in docs once this is merged You can [this](https://vercel.com/excalidraw/docs/7SNq14EEvhJPo6iBkFVtqX5BXvDc) for which build was ignored
author: ad1992

body:
> yeah once they are rebased with master after this is merged. Any concern with that ?
> 
> EDIT -> you are right this will start triggering for every PR's, I will add a check to make sure this triggers only for docs related changes

For what it's worth, by rebasing #2993 and #5449 I discovered a slight edge case.  That is, the `docs` build runs if pushing the rebase commit on a PR branched prior to #5482.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
> > yeah once they are rebased with master after this is merged. Any concern with that ?
> > EDIT -> you are right this will start triggering for every PR's, I will add a check to make sure this triggers only for docs related changes
> 
> For what it's worth, by rebasing #2993 and #5449 I discovered a slight edge case. That is, the `docs` build runs if pushing the rebase commit on a PR branched prior to #5482.

yeah, basically all active PRs will need to be merged/rebased on latest.

body:
Looking good!

- [x] It seems you've already added handlers for when hovering over the arrow points. I think we should add a hover state for the background as well, which will require adding it to appState in some form.
- [x] the line editor is visibly identical right now. Not sure what we wanna do about it long term, but we should differentiate somewhat. Maybe a different colors + size (slightly bigger?) for the line points when inside the editor?

author: dwelle

body:
some UX issues: 

- [x] clicking on the point shouldn't remove the hover effect

	![excal-arrow-point-click](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/182396540-9946458d-ef17-4395-849e-57d9a1ff3594.gif)

- [x] there's a flicker of the hovered point on pointerup (in the gif below it sometimes doesn't show for some reason — frame skip or something)

	![excal-arrow-point-drag-flicker](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/182397039-fc57ae3e-22b7-4750-a469-4fb1b2629e6e.gif)


- [x] after dragging on pointerup, and moving the cursor away, the hover effect remains

	![excal-arrow-point-hover-stuck](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/182396981-e5ef8014-aa64-4a88-8efc-a932153b1513.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
- [x] box-selecting a single linear element should set the `state.selectedLinearElement`
- [x] not necessarily a bug, but when the element is already selected without `state.selectedLinearElement` (which shouldn't happen after we fix all the bugs, but you can never be sure), doing some actions such as opening a contextmenu on it doesn't set the `state.selectedLinearElement` (which happens if you open contextmenu while the element _isn't_ already selected). May affect other cases.
- [x] I'm thinking that opening a contextmenu on a locked element (or locking it) shouldn't show the points since 1) the element isn't editable 2) it's not super noticeable. I'd consider rendering the normal bounding box when the element is locked.
- [x] when multiple elements selected, if you then click on one of the arrows part of that selection, it deselects everything (or rather, it selects the arrow, but doesn't set  `state.selectedLinearElement`, so it appears deselected)

	![excal-arrow-points-select-bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/182485370-5b9a04a8-a535-4c40-85d4-432135d0b70f.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
> not necessarily a bug, but when the element is already selected without state.selectedLinearElement (which shouldn't happen after we fix all the bugs, but you can never be sure), doing some actions such as opening a contextmenu on it doesn't set the state.selectedLinearElement (which happens if you open contextmenu while the element isn't already selected). May affect other cases.

Setting `selectedLinearElement` when opening context menu was fixed yesterday, its not working for you ?
author: ad1992

body:
> I'm thinking that opening a contextmenu on a locked element (or locking it) shouldn't show the points since 1) the element isn't editable 2) it's not super noticeable. I'd consider rendering the normal bounding box when the element is locked.

Showing bounding box is confusing since we are not showing it for arrows at all, I think its better not to show anything instead (pushed this change). 
Even showing bounding box for other shapes doesn't help in IMO, I would rather want to improve the locking behaviour overall and may be show a lock icon when locked elements are clicked which user can use to unlock, but thats a separate discussion
author: ad1992

body:
> Setting `selectedLinearElement` when opening context menu was fixed yesterday, its not working for you ?

I meant when the element is already selected without `selectedLinearElement` set. It shouldn't happen, but it can, so opening a contextmenu on an arrow should make sure it's set. That said, I guess we can ignore this for now and handle it during the selection handling rewrite.

> Showing bounding box is confusing since we are not showing it for arrows at all, I think its better not to show anything instead (pushed this change). Even showing bounding box for other shapes doesn't help in IMO, I would rather want to improve the locking behaviour overall and may be show a lock icon when locked elements are clicked which user can use to unlock, but thats a separate discussion

We're showing arrow's bounding box when multiple elements selected so why is bad to show it when a locked arrow is selected?

Whatever we end up doing with locking is unrelated, as we will still not want to show the non-editable points when a locked element is selected.
author: dwelle

body:
One other case I found:

- [x] select 2 elements and then deselect the 2nd one, leaving the arrow selected. `selectedLinearElement` state isn't set.

author: vercel

body:
@DanielJGeiger this is a good idea, but I'm hesitant to touch the user-intended order. After all, if the user wants the domain to be unsorted, let them (and it's not hard to sort beforehand in the spreadsheet). An example are descending sets ("e.g." what happens when we decrease the voltage in steps).

But I agree it's useful, especially for scatterplots. What about adding a checkbox `[ ] sort labels in ascending order` whenever we detect labels to be numerical?

And since it'd be opt-in, we can be even more forgiving and consider labels that successfully `parseFloat()`, so it works for scatterplots like these:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/181904335-fdc87741-c185-43a2-b325-f5ffb09c704e.png)
author: dwelle

body: Sadly All the shortcuts with "Option" in mac are breaking eg zenmode, view mode, toggling dark mode. Probably this is because its considering "Option key" combined with some other key as dead key when checking "event.key"
author: ad1992
jguddas

body:
> @ad1992 you are 100% right.
> 
> I don't have a solution how to correctly map the keys for cases where we use option, do you have any suggestions?
> 
> If not, let me know, and I will revert it for all the keybindings that use option.

Doesn't look like there are alternatives to handle dead keys if we want to use `event.key`, let's keep this PR open and we can update it once we have a better solution to handle option keys as well.

author: ad1992

body:
The goal is for the mid-point to be "phantom" until you click/drag on it. This way there's no actual point, unlike in current version where the point is always there, making the arrow 3-point from the start.

And in the future we may also allow adding more midpoints "forever" (until a given threshold).

Figma:

![figma-phantom-points](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/182818906-90aef565-eebb-4931-8582-110459d5b08c.gif)

We should also decide whether double-clicking on a point outside the editor should remove it.
author: dwelle

body:
Behavior is different if I drag the arrow (the mid point is added) and if I click, move the pointer and double click (the mid point is not added).

I agree with @dwelle that the mid-point should be invisible until used. If line sloppiness is cartoonist the mid-point breaks the continuity of the line which stylistically does not look very nice. 
I also agree that this would make sense if mid-points would continue to sub sections of the line. In its current form I don't yet see the value add of this feature, rather the contrary, because of the line break effect as shown on the video. If you want a multi-point line you can click-move-click-move-doubleclick.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/182838021-ae1d3b20-9f62-4f86-b207-98647fdf80f2.mp4

.

body: I'd decrease the bounding box offset from the line. For that matter, we may not do any special offset at first and gather feedback first. Also note that in a future PR we may wanna remove N/S/W/E handles as discussed previously, so it'll make it even less likely the points will conflict with the bounding box (except the start/end point might will be common, for one of them).
author: dwelle

body: Ok, so after further discussion, it seems we're in a bind and it's unclear how to resolve it: 1. making the bounding box align with the element's actual bounding box makes the points often collide with the resize handles which is not a great UX (and also doesn't look good) ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/183890331-a822792e-cd6b-4bd4-9e0a-caab96af3ed2.png) 2. offsetting the bounding box has its own host of issues: 1. doesn't look good either. The rendered bounding box is inconsistent to all the other elements. The expectation is broken. (in red what the actual bounding box should be) ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/183891378-e13ad291-3498-4206-bca9-e5c40bb25c88.png) 2. the resizing experience is weird. You go to the negative while your cursor is still inside the element: ![excal-linear-bounding-box-discrepancy](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/183891520-c2231d58-909e-4204-9453-bf1409a22cc9.gif) 3. grid aligning is affected / unintuitive ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/183891057-4443f05d-5549-4cb2-8636-846c4ae92a69.png) 3. keep the bounding box aligned, but offset the handles - solves 2.i and 2.iii, but not 2.ii ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/183894545-ab1338dc-6174-4e4a-be39-1b099fffa5bc.png) (note: the handles offset needs tweaking) 4. another option is to dynamically hide points that are close to nw/ne/sw/se resize handles (ignoring the s/w/n/e handles for now as they'll likely be removed soon from all elements). We may be doing something similar for points in general: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5556 5. Introduce slight padding for linear elements along with handles so it looks more neat as the points don't touch the bounding box. It will have the issues too but to lesser extent.

author: vercel

body:
@dwelle I have a few UX related questions:

1. should a text element or a bounding text be rotated when flipping vertically?
2. how does flipping with a shortcut or context menu should work:
   - flip a selection
   - flip elements individually inside a selection
   - have both options
author: alex-kim-dev

body:
Hey Alex!

Here's my thoughts.

For (2), it should take the center axis (for vertical flip, it would be a horizontal axis, and vice versa for horizontal flip) and mirror about that axis.

In practical terms it should do this (below is a vertical flip):

1. get common bounding box of selected elements (below, red dashed)
	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/113120547-fd7f2680-9211-11eb-897b-3a8e4bd04b71.png)

2. get the X axis going through center of that box
	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/113120561-0112ad80-9212-11eb-9a56-9e86060b2c7c.png)

3. for each element:
	1. mirror the position in relation to the center
			
		![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/113122157-a4b08d80-9213-11eb-94e0-d9d4ef0cb913.png)

	2. afterwards, flip the element individually
		![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/113122289-c27df280-9213-11eb-8547-8933d7645dfc.png)

The "axis" step is more of a intuition crutch, I suppose.

---

For (1), while it's technically correct to mirror the text, I would not do so — only their position + angle.

Here's an example of a vertical flip:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/198881716-853e4bab-311b-4254-a68f-66dc2f4ffa00.png)

Figma (for example) does fully mirror everything, but in the context of a drawing app such as Excalidraw I don't think it's useful.

So what we want to do with the text is center-axis-mirror the position, and a horizontal flip of its angle (the angle flip is what we're doing already in production with single text elements when flipping horizontally). Always horizontal flip no matter the flip type. This will mean that the text will never get upside down.

This goes counter to what we currently do in production when you flip text individually. IMO we should always do a horizontal flip there as well.

Same applies to text bound to an element — it should flip horizontally. In practical terms that means keep the angle identical to its bound component.
author: dwelle

body:
My current implementation works a bit differently with angles. Note the rotation handles on the gif, they are pointing the same direction after a vertical flip:

![Peek 2022-10-31 21-18](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45559664/199080823-41224257-2b0e-4833-92b4-4e6156bed4b8.gif)

Is it fine?
author: alex-kim-dev

body:
I ended up doing this without flipping angles. Also fixed bound text font size changing while resizing a shape. Next step is to fix tests, and I have a few questions:

1. for now every element is eligible for both horizontal & vertical flip, do I need to restrict this rule?
2. when performing a flip, I'm basically grabbing the NW handle and moving the pointer to the other side. There was a check in the old code for NE handle, if NW is not present. Is this still a problem? In which cases a selection couldn't have one of these?
3. should I resize/flip 2-point lines using `LinearElementEditor` or using `rescalePoints` is fine?

Update: the tests are actually expecting the angle flip
author: alex-kim-dev

body:
Thanks @alex-kim-dev!

> for now every element is eligible for both horizontal & vertical flip, do I need to restrict this rule?

No, except text, but that one it seems you're already accounting for!

> when performing a flip, I'm basically grabbing the NW handle and moving the pointer to the other side. There was a check in the old code for NE handle, if NW is not present. Is this still a problem? In which cases a selection couldn't have one of these?

Not sure, will have to check.

> Update: the tests are actually expecting the angle flip

🤔

---

Btw, the lines/arrows position flip correctly, but their position offset is not correct:

https://excalidraw-g444eznzb-excalidraw.vercel.app/#json=A9KAoiqK6cl7tMkXv1gFq,3IIF27AcV5pNRHnYmvU7ew

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/219803155-dadb19a7-c70b-4401-bf7a-3fad0f20dfca.png)
author: dwelle

body:
On the issue with different bounds for curve lines after flipping:

I discovered that roughjs is not able generate a perfectly mirrored shape (with roughness > 0), even if you pass the same options with the same seed value and a flipped set of `points`. The coordinates for `moveTo` operations remain the same as in non-flipped version, which results in different curves being generated.

To get a correctly mirrored curve line, one should change the sign of x or y coordinate for every operation in `ops`.

[sandbox with examples](https://stackblitz.com/edit/vitejs-vite-shm9jz)
author: alex-kim-dev

body:
> To get a correctly mirrored curve line, one should change the sign of x or y coordinate for every operation in `ops`.
> 
> [sandbox with examples](https://stackblitz.com/edit/vitejs-vite-shm9jz)

Nice. Does that affect how we render, calculate bounding box, hit-test etc.?

body: One more thing - does it matter if `x` or `y` are finite or not?
author: alex-kim-dev

body:
> One more thing - does it matter if `x` or `y` are finite or not?

Do you mean whether element/point positions should be finite (non-Infinity)? I think it's a good idea if they are, yes :).

Is there any specific reason why you're asking?
author: dwelle

body: Oh I meant integer, wrong word 😅
author: alex-kim-dev

body:
> Oh I meant integer, wrong word 😅

😂 

No, it's fine if they're not. Back then we were rounding/flooring as mad (and we still may be in some cases, though the plan is to remove all of that where possible), but not anymore. So float away!
author: dwelle

body:
Should multiple elements selection be rotated like in case with a single element?
![Peek 2022-08-24 10-17](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45559664/186335286-e855ebce-f089-4867-bea8-3da5161049e2.gif)
author: alex-kim-dev

body:
> Should multiple elements selection be rotated like in case with a single element? ![Peek 2022-08-24 10-17](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45559664/186335286-e855ebce-f089-4867-bea8-3da5161049e2.gif) [ ![Peek 2022-08-24 10-17](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45559664/186335286-e855ebce-f089-4867-bea8-3da5161049e2.gif) ](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45559664/186335286-e855ebce-f089-4867-bea8-3da5161049e2.gif) [ ](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/45559664/186335286-e855ebce-f089-4867-bea8-3da5161049e2.gif)

No, it should happen as you show in your gif — when elements are grouped, that is. When ungrouped, the common bounding box is not shown when rotating.

author: jcyh0120

body:
@alswl since you are adding "HanziPen SC" as a fallback for Virgil so will you be disabling virgil in order to use it? 
Also since its a system font, I think adding as a fallback under "normal" would be better option.
author: ad1992

body:
@ad1992 
> so will you be disabling virgil in order to use it?

No, I think the Virgil is a better style for English font, and "HanziPen SC fallback" solution will keeps others diagram looks the same in the solution.

> I think adding as a fallback under "normal"

As you see, the 'HanziPen SC" is a hand writing style, not a regular font style. I think this font is an alternative for Chinese font, not the alternative for "Normal".
author: alswl

body: Is it possible to add a handwriting chinese web font? HanziPen SC looks great but only works on system font. I cannot use it in ipad.
author: jcyh0120

body:
> Is it possible to add a handwriting chinese web font? HanziPen SC looks great but only works on system font. I cannot use it in ipad.

The cost of embedding font files is high. There are too many CJK fonts, which may require 5M-20M to store and transfer.
author: alswl

body:
> > Is it possible to add a handwriting chinese web font? HanziPen SC looks great but only works on system font. I cannot use it in ipad.
> 
> The cost of embedding font files is high. There are too many CJK fonts, which may require 5M-20M to store and transfer.

I see. I've tried installing the same font on my iphone and ipad but no luck. Seems that mobile browser doesn't apply the system Hanzipen SC font on portable devices. But it works on my macbook.

body: hope the pr merged
author: PhantomMaa

body:
> This is a instance http://excalidraw.alswl.com/ . Static // Support Chinese font // No Collaboration

Tried using this, but I don't get the handwritten font. Do I need to do something special? I'm on Windows, if that matters.
author: soliviantar

body:
@linguax-gc Maybe the Windows  Chinese handwriting is missing? I'm using `Kai` as name of 楷体。
According to https://xuui.net/ui-design/english-name-of-chinese-characters-table.html , I should fix to `KaiTi`.
author: alswl

body:
@alswl I have KaiTi on my system, so maybe it's because of the name you used for the font, yes. I just tried on mobile (Android) and it doesn't work either. So, unfortunately, your solution is not viable immediately on all platforms, which I think it's a problem. I don't believe Android has a handwritten font included in the system fonts. Not sure about iOS, but going by what @jcyh0120 said above it doesn't either.

In any case, HanziPen SC looks suitable for the Excalidraw aesthetic, but I'm not so sure about KaiTi. I use it for documents, but I don't believe it fits with the sketchy visuals of Excalidraw.

I believe, for the public website, another solution might be needed. Maybe having an option to manually activate the Virgil with Xiaolai as fallback font (#5370 ) and have it downloaded and activated if the user wishes to? Or maybe automatically loading it when the language of the site is changed to Chinese?

I understand the size of the file (14 mb) might be a problem for some connections, but using normal squary fonts as it is now is certainly not working visually.

As I said on the other issue I ended up compiling an offline version of Excalidraw on Docker and replacing the original Virgil font with the one with fallback to XiaoLai, so I can finally work with handwritten font on Chinese, but having the feature on the public website is the most desirable outcome, obviously.

Hope @dwelle doesn't mind my mentioning them to ask about the devs' thoughts on this issue.🙏
author: soliviantar

body:
@linguax-gc can you try this one in your Windows? This is a version using `KaiTi` instead of `Kai`.
https://e2da098c.excalidraw-alswl-com.pages.dev/
author: alswl
soliviantar
alswl

body:
@alswl Checked again and still nothing. I'm on Windows 11. I looked at my installed fonts and the 楷体 font is called like that in Word, for example, but the ttf name is SIMKAI.TTF, and there's another one called STKAITI.TTF. Both provide the standard Kai calligraphy script.

I still think it doesn't really suit the aesthetics of Excalidraw. Wouldn't it be possible to add Xiaolai (https://github.com/lxgw/kose-font) as the font family, but instruct the user to install it on their system instead of embedding it? (Although embedding is, apparently, the only solution for mobile devices.)
author: soliviantar

body:
> the ttf name is SIMKAI.TTF, and there's another one called STKAITI.TTF

Thanks, I think the new Windows version using the new hand-writing fonts.

> instruct the user to install it on their system instead of embedding

This requirement is beyond the scope of this pull request and I think you can draft a new pull request.

body:
- [x] start/end points should always show no matter what:

	![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/186398955-458e1429-7f57-44af-948c-4cf123274cdb.png)

- [x] IMO we shouldn't hide a point while dragging. In fact, I'd probably not hide points at all until you _deselect_ the element, so there's no points disappearing under your cursor, and you don't get into a place where you can't drag the point away after it gets hidden.

	![excal-dynamic-points-1](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/186403609-437cbd4c-de69-4ba7-a8be-90c515df737b.gif)

- [ ] hidings points should be "stable". Right now it seems to behave "take a segment of two points, if they're too close, hide the one on the right", which results in this behavior where a single point can be hidden/visible based on proximity to prev/next point. I'd make it "every odd-index point is always visible; hide even-index points as needed instead"

	![excal-dynamic-points-2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/186403526-968dc34d-da0d-452c-a901-ae2c75865835.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
- [x] the visible indices should be recalculated on points being added/removed:

	![excal-dynamic-points-3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/186880264-5ec0cc6d-0ffc-419a-be58-e6706850eab0.gif)

- [x] should take into account current zoom (as such, visible indices should also be recalculated on zoom change?)

	![excal-dynamic-points-4-zoom](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/186880580-efb11d7f-4d74-4bc3-8cab-081bea47e5fb.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
>  should take into account current zoom (as such, visible indices should also be recalculated on zoom change?)

Zooming issue is in prod too so for points inside and outside editor as well, can raise a separate PR for it, and I guess we are tracking it in a diff issue as well. As per `visible` indices, I think its ok not to calculate on zoom change as it could be confusing ?
author: ad1992

body:
- [x] dragging invisible points shouldn't be allowed:
 
  ![excal-dynamic-points-5-drag-hidden](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/187497498-a6b1cdaf-38c4-4e1e-afc5-0e6824f391f3.gif)

- [x] should hide the N-1 point if last two points too close together

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/187499973-ba6f21d0-45d4-4030-8fb7-4ece82eed037.png)

- [ ] question: should we move points together when dragging a point near to an invisible point(s)?
author: dwelle

body:
> should we move points together when dragging a point near to an invisible point(s)?

You mean invisible point will be dragged without user knowing about it ? No we shouldn't do that imo.

body:
I've updated some of the tests. I assume that `expect(renderScene).toHaveBeenCalledTimes()` decreased by 1 because the test canvas is zero in width and updateDOMRect did not call setState.

I am not 100% sure about this though...
author: zsviczian

body: If the root cause is `renderScene()` throwing for `0`-width/height canvases, then let's add the check to `renderScene()` instead (line 330 is a good candidate).
author: dwelle

body: so that was my first attempt, however, then I got a different error... which is why I decided to attack the problem at the trigger point, it seemed to me like a cleaner solution.
author: zsviczian

body: shouldn't Excalidraw be unmounted  when switching the tab instead?
author: ad1992

body: You can have multiple tabs open next to one another (visible). Checking to unmount and remount when the tab is hidden and when it reappears I guess is possible, but I don't have Obsidian events for that. Re-loading Excalidraw when clicking through tabs would also feel laggier and introduce a number of other complexities.
author: zsviczian

body:
> so that was my first attempt, however, then I got a different error... which is why I decided to attack the problem at the trigger point, it seemed to me like a cleaner solution.

What error did you get? The problem with not updating domRect is that there may be (in the future if not today) other code paths. 

We'll also be decoupling the renderer soon, so host apps will be able to render separately.

As such, if the root cause is canvas not being able to render to 0 dimensions, then we should fix this in the renderer, not the editor.

body: I think the hiding based on proximity behavior from the other PR should apply here as well. Is the logic reusable and thus merge this now and introduce the point hiding after we merge the other PR, or do we need to implement separately?
author: dwelle

body:
> I think the hiding based on proximity behavior from the other PR should apply here as well. Is the logic reusable and thus merge this now and introduce the point hiding after we merge the other PR, or do we need to implement separately?

It can be reused but I think inside editor its ok to show all the points, as thats what we are doing even for 2 point arrows (inside editor we are not hiding in that PR) as inside editor user should be able to edit all points.
Later if we want we can hide inside editor.
As per this PR, what I can try is not show mid points unless segment length is > x ?

body: Oh, I didn't mean existing points, but the phantom points. If there's too little space between two existing points, let's not suggest a phantom point as it unlikely you'll want to create one in there anyway.

body: Midpoints in curve is not improved Earlier I was always taking `t = 0.5` which might not be true always depending on the nature of the curve (See left image), now the approach is different, I am trying to find the actual `t` on the curve for a given length (50% of the curve length in case of midpoint)
author: ad1992

body:
Works great ❤️.

Haven't fully reviewed the math, which may or may not be connected to the perf issue pointed above. Solution is either use better math / heuristic to not compute as many points as you're doing right now, or caching.
author: dwelle

body:
- [x] In specific cases it creates the new point in a wrong segment (not related to caching). From what I gathered the conditions are 1) at least one phantom mid-point is hidden due to proximity 2) there's at last one more segment after the segment in which you're adding the mid-point

  ![excal-midpoints-wrong-new-point](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/189680617-29f3919e-2b47-4df7-bd3f-1f162a72efdf.gif)
  
  ```
  {"type":"excalidraw/clipboard","elements":[{"type":"line","version":761,"versionNonce":84824830,"isDeleted":false,"id":"DuAsvTnKAEwdo-LDPKnhG","fillStyle":"hachure","strokeWidth":1,"strokeStyle":"solid","roughness":1,"opacity":100,"angle":0,"x":635,"y":221,"strokeColor":"#000000","backgroundColor":"transparent","width":226,"height":156,"seed":2074270108,"groupIds":[],"strokeSharpness":"sharp","boundElements":[],"updated":1662992934399,"link":null,"locked":false,"startBinding":null,"endBinding":null,"lastCommittedPoint":null,"startArrowhead":null,"endArrowhead":null,"points":[[0,0],[156,-86],[176,-77],[226,21],[66,70]]},{"type":"rectangle","version":30,"versionNonce":1475535842,"isDeleted":false,"id":"51WkiFZ4vNg5vQnWvfDk1","fillStyle":"hachure","strokeWidth":1,"strokeStyle":"solid","roughness":1,"opacity":100,"angle":0,"x":786,"y":173,"strokeColor":"#c92a2a","backgroundColor":"transparent","width":120,"height":42,"seed":1518143535,"groupIds":[],"strokeSharpness":"sharp","boundElements":[],"updated":1662992934399,"link":null,"locked":false}],"files":{}}
  ```

- midpoints position not being updated in many cases. Some I found:
  - [x] during collaboration when someone moves your line while you're in the editor 
  - [x] you update settings that change the line's shape (e.g. edge sharpness or sloppiness)
  - [x] undo/redo: e.g. you create a mid-point and undo it, or you move a point and undo the change
  - [x] duplicating a point: 1) select a point 2) cmd-D (or use the duplicate button in sidebar)

author: ad1992

mabdullahadeel

body:
> @ad1992 I still don't get it. why just changing `UIOptions.canvasActions.theme` to `UIOptions.canvasActions.toggleTheme` caused this issue?
> 
> Just to be sure, I renamed `toggleTheme` back to `theme` and everything was working as expected 😂 (theme: `undefined`, UIOptions: `undefined`).

Yeah this was a tricky bug, the issue was due to [this](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/actions/manager.tsx#L86) condition evaluating to false and theme button not being rendered as`canvasActions["toggleTheme"]` was null 
```
(action.name in canvasActions
            ? canvasActions[action.name as keyof typeof canvasActions]
            : true) 
```            
The reason it was working in previous commit was because the action was `toggleTheme` but the canvasActions prop was still `them` hence this condition was always evaluating to true as the canvasActions didn't have any prop  `toggleTheme`
author: ad1992

> 
> ```
> (action.name in canvasActions
>             ? canvasActions[action.name as keyof typeof canvasActions]
>             : true) 
> ```
> 
> The reason it was working in previous commit was because the action was `toggleTheme` but the canvasActions prop was still `them` hence this condition was always evaluating to true as the canvasActions didn't have any prop `toggleTheme`

Hah, I just went down the same rabbit hole to figure this out.

I'm not sure we should be adding this implicit logic there — it's really hidden and will bite us again as it did here.

I'll think on what to do later, for now I've removed the `showThemeBtn` which is now redundant.
author: dwelle

body: @mabdullahadeel thanks for the PR and patience! 🚀 ❤️

author: dwelle

body:
Looks like this PR is focused solely on z-index actions (Send forward, Bring backward, etc..).

But it seems like there is another bug in reconciliation logic, which moves an element spliced somewhere in the middle of `Scene.elements` to the end. [See this comment for more details](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5742#issuecomment-1272382423) 
It was hard to reproduce because it happens at full scene sync interval, which is every 20 seconds right now. If you set `SYNC_FULL_SCENE_INTERVAL_MS` to a short duration (like 1s), the issue becomes much more apparent.

body:
> I do not have the patience to dig deeper to understand why this is called twice, but if I abort the second time, the issue is resolved.

It's scheduled twice because we update the state multiple times / in multiple sites, and the updates aren't batched (or there are consecutive updates).

The long-term solution is a better state management.

We can merge, thanks!

author: urmilparikh

body: Hey, so we need to decide whether we want wrapping for arrow with labels ? Below are few options 1. Wrap it after a threshold but don't wrap during resize (This is similar to how Miro does). Also do we need wrapping during resize ? Resize https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11256141/195878341-25c3f2a5-b60f-4711-b9d4-10b98ccddfaf.mp4 2. Wrap it always. This would have the same limitation as above but its just it would always wrap whenever container dimensions changes (This is similar to how Eraser does but in addition to that it also changes text position which we probably want to avoid even if we wrap) https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11256141/195879847-88244ce6-58d9-4052-9e99-91c9fc6332ec.mp4 3. Don't wrap at all (This would be similar to how tldraw does it) https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11256141/195880451-e8eedc40-b137-43be-9b64-d66128d3890d.mp4 We could go with (3) and introduce wrapping later but if we want to introduce it later we can instead introduce it right away and improve for vertical lines like cases later. I think wrapping does make it look nicer and I myself would need it often as a user. Would love to know your thoughts before we make the final decision so please put your thoughts below which option looks the best and why? Also if you have any other suggestion, do share :)

author: ad1992

body:
Rethinking the discussion of only supporting labels for arrows -

I think we should keep both, the main reason being the arrowheads could be removed or added later depending on the use case so it might be ambiguous when the label is kept even if the arrowhead is removed but not allowed to add a label to a new line. So to keep it simple and avoid any such ambiguities I think we should keep in both

cc @dwelle @maielo
author: ad1992

body:
> I think we should keep both, the main reason being the arrowheads could be removed or added later depending on the use case so it might be ambiguous when the label is kept even if the arrowhead is removed but not allowed to add a label to a new line. So to keep it simple and avoid any such ambiguities I think we should keep in both

The main goal here is to keep lines as magic-less as possible because lines are used to draw complex shapes.

As we've discussed on the call, I don't think there's much use case for adding labels to lines. I'd ship without it. It's better to not have a support for something and add it based on feedback, rather than remove it later. It's also more likely we'll get feedback if the feature is "missing" and people expect it to be there (if that's the case).

- [x] probably better not to wrap in these cases at all (it'll still be suboptimal behavior, but better. And ultimately we want the text to move above the arrow.):

  ![excal-labeled-arrows-vertical-noodle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/202306061-f579d123-a229-4237-b7cc-c098126bc22a.gif)

- [ ] UX: double-clicking on center to add text (which will be common as that's usually the behavior when you use a mouse to create text) always creates a midpoint currently. That's not a good behavior, especially since it's very hard to delete points (but that's a different issue):

  ![excal-labeled-arrows-double-click-creates-point](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/202306640-fb2d4d03-af34-4810-aa13-86ec01590831.gif)

  We'll likely need to make midpoint creation to not work on click, but rather on drag (with a threshold of ~10px).
author: dwelle

body:
- [ ] some arrows manifest a buggy behavior where selecting the arrow immediately deselects it. Steps:

  1. load https://excalidraw-2ks4r1ul0-excalidraw.vercel.app/#json=cmT6LFyxNFFwz7QS0xAYy,i-dI8zjclu0djIjMS5w0sA
  2. double-click the arrow (or `enter`) to edit text
  3. deselect the arrow by clicking elsewhere on canvas
  4. try to select the arrow again, by clicking on the arrow itself (not the text)
  
  ![excal-labeled-arrows-deselection-bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/202412035-c1b8be27-e940-49dc-93a2-c231299bee2a.gif)
author: dwelle

body: As per the above, do you mean disable wrapping when resizing? or increase the threshold for wrapping ? Since the width is changing so it should wrap as per the available width, even if we move it above the arrow then also we should wrap when the width gets updated until a threshold is reached.
author: ad1992

body:
> UX: double-clicking on center to add text (which will be common as that's usually the behavior when you use a mouse to create text) always creates a midpoint currently. That's not a good behavior, especially since it's very hard to delete points (but that's a different issue):

Yeah even now in prod this conflicts with the line editor so this needs to be fixed anyway.  Will move to drag until a certain threshold for adding midpoints.
author: ad1992

body:
- [x] one other bug: the cursor gets stuck when resizing over the midpoint

    ![excal-arrow-labels-drag-stuck](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/203661256-ea34159f-4794-48f8-b957-337ef7bc33de.gif)
author: dwelle

body:
- [x] update tooltip message (`cmd-enter`) & help dialog shrotcuts to reflect the line editor changes
- [ ] while duplicating via Option/Alt-drag, I managed to end up in this state:
  
    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204620714-c3059007-888f-4aef-b585-25c020481470.png)

    Cannot reproduce anymore, but diffing the pre & post elements, it looks like the text was deleted (apart from `x`/`y` change which seems weird too):

    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204621110-2137d300-3b2f-400b-b837-152c371a605d.png)

- [x] the editor padding is different when creating vs after you commit. The editor should have the same padding (= larger). Works fine if editing an already existing label.

    ![excal-arrow-labels-creating-editor-size-diff](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204633216-a920a292-1ab2-40f0-b6d2-75941efae224.gif)

- [x] we should get rid of the flicker when creating a 4th+ point. It seems as if the text repositions 2x. First, incorrectly. This suggests that it may be dependent on subsequent renders as you move the cursor. It should happen at once.

    ![excal-arrow-labels-reflow-flicker](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204618399-33a7e86b-7d10-4b9a-9a29-2c70938540af.gif)

- [ ] typing in editor sometimes increases the mask before the text actually wraps:

    ![excal-arrow-labels-editor-mask-issue](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204633726-983e2fa5-a60e-4576-a256-f2951e642adf.gif)

- [x] label is not reflowed (wrapped) when the arrow size/angle is being changed due to changing bound container position:

    ![excal-arrow-labels-text-not-reflowing-when-moving-binding-container](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204634336-44c2d744-aee6-48ce-9409-4aa66095f229.gif)

- [x] I'm sure this affects other types of elements in prod, but let's start fixing it now: when duplicating a labeled arrow, the new arrow's (or the old one's? not sure) `boundElements` array is now populated with a duplicate text binding.

----

- horizontal arrows allow for the text to be too long IMO, making the text cover most of the arrow:

    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204619134-ec0330e3-b941-419b-beff-693ef9f91c93.png)

    This is also strange when you consider that diagonal/vertical arrows wrap text much sooner, while the text technically has more space when arrow vertical:

    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204619387-f951a74a-bfbf-4dc7-827f-aab07b2516cf.png)
author: dwelle

body:
>  horizontal arrows allow for the text to be too long IMO, making the text cover most of the arrow:

What's wrong with this? The text starts wrapping when it crosses max width (leaving ~30px) from both sides of the arrow.

As per vertical / dioganal dimensions, its not possible to predict the max  dimensions coz of width being too small so I have set a min width of 300px when width being too small, we can increase that min width but I think 300px is fine and can be modified later / we can improve the wrapping for non horizontal arrows later but not something which is needed as of now. Right now the behaviour is close to what Eraser is doing.
author: ad1992

body:
> > horizontal arrows allow for the text to be too long IMO, making the text cover most of the arrow:
> 
> What's wrong with this? The text starts wrapping when it crosses max width (leaving ~30px) from both sides of the arrow.
> 
> As per vertical / dioganal dimensions, its not possible to predict the max dimensions coz of width being too small so I have set a min width of 300px when width being too small, we can increase that min width but I think 300px is fine and can be modified later / we can improve the wrapping for non horizontal arrows later but not something which is needed as of now. Right now the behaviour is close to what Eraser is doing.

Yeah, it's not a blocker. Just wanted to point out it's likely not ideal. I'll remove the todo for now.
author: dwelle

body:
>  we should get rid of the flicker when creating a 4th+ point. It seems as if the text repositions 2x. First, incorrectly. This suggests that it may be dependent on subsequent renders as you move the cursor. It should happen at once.

Again a cache issue :p

body:
Hi, @dwelle, I found out that there was also a problem with multi point line elements, in particular when the curve exceeded the min/max points, the element had an offset which was the difference between the last point and the end of the curve, as it can be seen in the video below:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50418432/196042175-ce7a5f32-94fe-4e20-9417-f986a1def5df.mov

After I managed to solve this issue, another minor issue arised: when roughness is > 1 the flipping is not pixel perfect but there is a slight imprecision due to the roughness expanding the line. As it can be seen in the video:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/50418432/196042336-db8d0511-e60f-4fb8-8cd9-77ad739fafd7.mov
