author: vercel

body:
Boom! 💥

Playing with it and loving it.

Some notes & points for discussion for both you @barnabasmolnar and for Tony, you'll need to sort it out :). The design points we can discuss offline.

- The welcome screen:
  - [x] should have `pointer-events: none` except for the actionable buttons which will reset the `pointer-events`. Right now interacting with the canvas is blocked by it, as well as other things like pasting.
  - [x] Maybe the contract (especially in dark mode) is too low after all. TBD.

- UI too large on FHD (1080px) screen (and lower?). Looks 🔥 on QHD (2K) screen. Prob needs 36px sizing for the toolbar on FHD, via @maielo 

- sidebar: 
  - [x] swap the button groups position  

    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197363207-a848db65-b5a1-4faa-b6ff-7c953fa291b6.png)
  
  - [x] shadow maybe too strong? Plus it should revert to regular shadow on dock.
    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197363341-65b72818-fecf-4565-a4d1-21437ed933c4.png)

  - [x] don't display library button when library sidebar is docked
    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197363374-9e300cef-e792-4e3d-a3ed-aa7bd4811453.png)

- [x] the loading state for library is not great. The spinner renders in wrong places (and in different places depending on whether something is already in library or not) and also results in layout shift.

- [x] language select
  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197363304-7fd899be-36a7-4d8b-b5c3-8b5b91bb94bb.png)

- arrowheads
  - [x] no active state (same as production 🤔)
  - [x] the popup buttons are lighter gray than regular buttons for some reason. Hard to read esp in dark mode.
    ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197363775-48bc1744-b355-43af-914a-ee11f592316d.png)
  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197363817-49f7f89b-e7c1-4989-83ba-d973b92d99ae.png)

- [x] align buttons when 2 elements are selected incorrectly flow to the wrong row. I.e. they should not mingle.

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197363899-9cb382d2-304a-4408-bfb6-c90390732df1.png)
  
  production:
  
  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197363905-2c7b2b24-972b-43c3-8702-99fc005e0215.png)

- [x] Padding difference between `Stroke → Stroke Width` and `Stroke → background`. Also causing visual layout shift when selecting items.

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197364132-e0b2e7f7-c47f-41d3-84f6-e4c12c010c3f.png) ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197364133-ae0ef0ff-43a4-43f7-a492-d693159420a8.png)

- [x] The bottom right buttons: not sure the switch to vertical stacking makes sense here as we don't use it anywhere else.

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197364240-37f2dd79-a148-486d-b013-89d77cbde874.png)

- [x] incorrect color of the hints in dark mode (too pronounced vs light mode)

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197364315-bc4ef661-ef27-40dd-af2a-1888ae651a5e.png)
author: dwelle

body:
Super 🔥 🔥 🔥 !!!

Extremely looking forward to ship this :) :) 

What i found apart what @dwelle already mentioned

1. [x] weird jumping of toolbar when opening and closing libs 
2. [x] when refreshing page flickering "splash screen"
3. [x] 36px for tool icons on FHD.
4. [x] wrong font (arial, should be assistant, at least in hamburger menu) - https://fonts.google.com/specimen/Assistant?vfquery=assista
5. [x] hamburger menu items are too large on FHD and less resolution - per design it should have 32px height, right now it has 39px;
6.  we need to figure out how to allow host apps to change splashscreen (talking about plus), hamburger menu items (plus again)
7. i am going to give you real text for splashscreen 

9. [x] can't paste images when splashscreen is on
10. [x] ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8983870/197411617-a4a1d996-3cae-4942-8338-d7d5e8bd1dbd.png)
12. [x] new bouning box
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8983870/197508591-fe856c27-af48-4403-9eb5-acfe516ecf81.png)
author: maielo

body:
I don't have much feedback as most of it has already been written by @maielo and @dwelle. 

But my main question is - wouldn't it be better to have a Live collaboration button next to the hamburger menu? I don't know, but for some reason it doesn't feel right to have it next to the Libraries on the right side. Maybe it's because I'm used to the current version where it's also on the left side. But maybe that's why we should keep it there. And yes, I didn't realize this before 😃 I had to see it live.

> would use "Start collaboration" ??? what others think?

@maielo Well, considering most of the other actions (maybe all of them) in hamburger menu are named with a "verb", it would make sense .. But people are used to Live collaboration, so ....
author: gabrielamacakova

body:
> But my main question is - wouldn't it be better to have a Live collaboration button next to the hamburger menu? I don't know, but for some reason it doesn't feel right to have it next to the Libraries on the right side.

Yeah, I'm not against putting it to the left side. The argument was that most apps put it on the right so that's what people expect. I didn't do any research myself so can't say how prevalent it is.

But, I would also not rule out putting the "export to image" button next to hamburger as it's quite a common action.
author: dwelle

body: This looks amazing 🔥🔥, great work @barnabasmolnar ❤️ A few points in addition to what already addressed above 1. The popup would be better to move below like it is now in the current version so it doesn't block the hex code, in case I want to update hex after opening the popup 2. [x] In dark mode I feel the splash screen font color is not that visible so may be lighten it a bit so its more readable Using a version lighter it looks better (var(--color-gray-70)) 4. How about putting the lock button on the right side instead of the left since in mobile we show it right next to the eraser ? so position will almost remain same when device dimensions changes 5. Shouldn't we use the primary color shade when selecting in context menu ? 6. As per the edges I think the dotted lines are making it look more similar so we might want to remove the dotted lines to make it clear or improve the edges in the icons so its more distinct (The prod is more clear) 7. Should we rename "load" to "Import" ? I think its more intuitive, or maybe "Open" but to align with "export", "import" would sound better. port" ? 8. I misunderstood this as sharing the read-only link of excalidraw eg in social accounts. We should use the collab icon of people which we use inside the hamburger menu as well Also, I like "Start collaboration" more - probably because that's I am used to and when it comes to collaborations it's going to be live so maybe putting live is redundant. So "Start" looks better to me. 9. Should we also add a link to discord in the help dialog? 10. Some suggestions regarding the hamburger menu ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11256141/197716782-eba18183-78b5-48b8-ba8b-8568a7550d31.png) Also we should add twitter account as well in this section 11. Let's add tooltips for undo/redo. Later we should move to using our tooltip everywhere.
author: ad1992

body:
This looks great!  I am glad to see it moving forward.

My main comment pertains to additional suggestions for improving the current editor.  When in mobile form factor (eg side-by-side desktop windows), the element properties panel can fill most of the window, obscuring the selected element(s).  A keyboard shortcut to hide the props panel, without finalizing the editing element, could also help.

For what it's worth, this PR is mostly orthogonal to my main PR (#2993) in terms of merge conflicts.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
Also in the current editor, pressing the properties button while editing a text element finalizes the text, without opening the properties panel.  Besides finalizing the text, the text element further becomes unselected.

By "mobile form factor", I mean having Excalidraw open side-by-side with another window on the desktop.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
> My main comment pertains to additional suggestions for improving the current editor. When in mobile form factor (eg side-by-side desktop windows), the element properties panel can fill most of the window, obscuring the selected element(s). A keyboard shortcut to hide the props panel, without finalizing the editing element, could also help.

Yep. The mobile redesign will come later, we haven't really touched it in this one (apart from using the same styleguide).

> Also in the current editor, pressing the properties button while editing a text element finalizes the text, without opening the properties panel. Besides finalizing the text, the text element further becomes unselected.

Cannot repro. IMO it works the same as current production?
author: dwelle

body:
There's also a few things with the WelcomeScreen we should fix:

- [x] some basic RTL support (e.g. when you pick an arabic language):

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197893827-f1068982-91c8-40e1-bc90-e8f59c90974e.png)

- [x] the welcome screen should account for screen sizes. Maybe hide the arrows and keep the center. If even the center doesn't fit, hide it as well? On mobile we can show the center if it fits, as well.

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197894009-ea009378-65e7-400c-b553-3577774583e5.png)

- [x] Also, we need to put all labels (not just welcomeScreen labels) into `en.json` fo i18n (and use `{t("path.to.translation")}`)

- [ ] there are some missing `:active` states, mainly in library buttons. Maybe `:hover` too. Will check properly tomorrow.

- [ ] align `:focus` outlines in the sidebar buttons and sidebar popups (I think there are ~3 different ones). And we should probably use `:focus-visible` so it doesn't show for non-keyboard interaction. We can leave the latter for future PRs though.
author: dwelle

body:
> Also, we need to put all labels (not just welcomeScreen labels) into en.json fo i18n (and use {t("path.to.translation")})

They are. I even wrote the Hungarian translations for them. 🇭🇺 😃 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5780/files#diff-b1791c7a47875ce85f2f1e57ef6c25a9b993660f9bcc840d8f21daac6161b5a0
author: barnabasmolnar

body:
> They are. I even wrote the Hungarian translations for them. 🇭🇺

ok, nvm. I don't know why I (wrongly) assumed instead checking, sorry! But, I don't know if Crowdin will catch the HU translations, or it needs it to be done from their UI. We'll see!
author: dwelle

body:
Moving the unresolved TODOs from previous comments (mine & @maielo's) here.

---

- [x] ⚠⚠⚠ collab button needs active state when collaborating (similar to current production)

- [ ] Seeing this in practice, the visual difference is actually kinda weird. I'm not sure if it really makes sense. Also I'm still not super keen on the island shadows.

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197364226-75d53b80-ef6b-440f-88fe-6b64034819af.png)

---

Not blocking. But needs some thought and review later:

- sidebar: the added friction of hiding some of the context buttons in the dropdown is still "too damn  high"

- [ ] arrowheads: still not sure about the the popup blending too much with the sidebar:

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/198146507-9e410c2a-6d3f-40ca-a596-ea0325213c5c.png)

- [ ] I'm not quite convinced the b/w colors convey information as for what is the selected element (without having to inspect closely). I'll let other comment on this.

  IMO we won't do anything with this because "it looks good", but just for the record.

  ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/197364187-20a76e26-54ca-4ebc-9686-1a8e8b26fec5.png)

---

Ad @ad1992's points:

> The popup would be better to move below like it is now in the current version so it doesn't block the hex code, in case I want to update hex after opening the popup

We'll be redesigning the color picker as per Figma soon so it doesn't matter.

>  How about putting the lock button on the right side instead of the left since in mobile we show it right next to the eraser ? so position will almost remain same when device dimensions changes

Mobile UI is orthogonal here I'd say, and we'll be redesigning it anyway. But There may be an arugment made. Let's keep it as is for now so we don't ship too many "breaking changes" to the UI and we'll see later.

> Shouldn't we use the primary color shade when selecting in context menu ? 

Contextmenu needs a complete overhaul after we ship this.

> As per the edges I think the dotted lines are making it look more similar so we might want to remove the dotted lines to make it clear or improve the edges in the icons so its more distinct (The prod is more clear)

Hard agree. The only salient argument to ignore this is that you won't really be looking at the buttons after you do it once or twice, and simpyl switch to the other one. But still not a good argument to keep it I think.

> Should we rename "load" to "Import" ? I think its more intuitive, or maybe "Open"  but to align with "export", "import" would sound better.

I'd go with "Open", maybe.

> I misunderstood this as sharing the read-only link of excalidraw eg in social accounts. We should use the collab icon of people which we use inside the hamburger menu as well

- [x] I agree here as well. Suppoedly it's the standard even for collab, but I'm not sure. We'll need to revisit this.

> Should we also add a link to discord in the help dialog?

Help dialog will need a complete overhaul as well.
author: dwelle

body: We can actually still only handle the `paste` event, we just need to know whether shift was pressed which we can't do from the `ClipboardEvent`. So we need to set a flag in the `keydown` event, which occurs before `paste`. It's kinda hacky, but AFAIK not much else we can do (apart from using the async clipboard API as you did, which isn't ideal).

author: vjeux

body:
Pushed a few changes: 

- remove changelog. We only write changelog for package-related changes. The rest is autogenerated from the commit log.
- reverted inlining of `probablySupportsClipboardReadText`. Tests keep working fine. Otherwise, the course of action would be to try 1) mock tests, or 2) make the `probablySupportsClipboardReadText` into a callable function, instead of inlining, which is error prone if we used that check elsewhere.
- changed how we handle plaintext pasting, so that it doesn't prevent pasting images or elements

I've also changed the line spacing a bit:

- keep spacing consistent regardless of zoom. We don't want to increase/decrease spacing just because you happen to be zoomed in/out.
- add at most one empty line between elements — I've read the comments above, but I think we should not keep multiple empty lines even if in source text. Again, it's about consistency (similar to how e.g. CommonMark handles whitespace) and so that if you copy/paste a spreadsheet or similar data with potentially lots of empty lines, there won't be a huge unnecessary holes. If people show up with counter use cases, we can revert back.
- instead of always using a default line height for spacing, make line space account for current line height. For example, when you paste emoji, the line height is bigger, so the space show be bigger.

Plus some refactor and other small tweaks.

> I have realized that this would not be ideal if we could paste from another source other than key-pressing. That would mean that we should remember every time to set that variabile before calling the pasteFromClipboard function.

We should do both actually. The setTimeout IMO is slightly redundant if we reset on each paste, though as you say it won't cover possible future cases of non-keyboard plaintext-pasting so I kept it.

body:
That fix in actionFlip was a bit cryptic, and it also must have broken something because some of the tests I added were failing.

I didn't investigate further, because I made the fix inside the `src/element/resizeElements.ts` instead, I'm calculating whether the original `X2`/`Y2` point crossed the center of the element during the resize. Coincidentally fixes when manually flipping by resizing from the center as well (by `Alt/Opt-dragging`).

It's actually pretty cryptic as well because the variable names in that function are super wrong and unintuitive. Will need to be rewritten at some point, but I didn't have time now.

body: Here, `Ctrl`+`+/-` (on the keyboard) zooms by 10% consistently.  However, `Ctrl`+`Wheel` zooms by more than 10% when the zoom is > 100%.  Is that intended?
author: DanielJGeiger

body: @DanielJGeiger That is a good question. It would probably be best to fix that as well, or at least make it increase by multiples of ZOOM_STEP. I'd vote for ZOOM_STEP increments at any zoom level. I'll take a look at the code.
author: zsviczian

body:
> Here, `Ctrl`+`+/-` (on the keyboard) zooms by 10% consistently. However, `Ctrl`+`Wheel` zooms by more than 10% when the zoom is > 100%. Is that intended?

IIRC this was intentional to make keyboard usage more precise/deliberate.

If we are to align at all (and I'm not sure we should), then it should be the other way round to what @zsviczian suggest (if I'm reading it right): ditch linear increasing (what keyboard is using) and move to non-linear (what wheel is using).

Checking Miro, Figma, tldraw, each is doing it differently so there doesn't seem to be any standard out there.

---

Anyway, since there's no functional different from production, we can merge the PR 👍

body:
@behnammodi Good idea, but I have 2 notes on the code:

1. it must be done on the app-level, not the editor component level. So put the title update in the `src/excalidraw-app/index.tsx` same as we do currently.
  
  I haven't checked what will need to be done for you to get the `fileHandle` outside the editor. To mimic what you're doing currently, you can put the `document.title` update into `onChange()` — but I'd want to throttle it or make it conditional based on title change only (I don't feel comfortable updating the title on every change).

2. the title should still contain the app name, so something like
  
    ```
    document.title = fileHandle?.name ? `{fileHandle.name} - {APP_NAME}` : APP_NAME
    ```
author: dwelle

body:
I found it very convenient for `X` to be bonded to the pen tool.
Especially when using the right hand for a pen tablet and the left hand for shortcuts like `E` to erase, and `X` to draw.
Now I have to look at the keyboard each time to use the new shortcut, it's a regression for me.

Why old shortcut can't be left along with the new one?
author: adrianbienias

body:
> I found it very convenient for `X` to be bonded to the pen tool. Especially when using the right hand for a pen tablet and the left hand for shortcuts like `E` to erase, and `X` to draw. Now I have to look at the keyboard each time to use the new shortcut, it's a regression for me.
> 
> Why old shortcut can't be left along with the new one?

`x` shortcut for freedraw tool is non-standard, and we may potentially want to use it for something else  in the future.

For now, I've reintroduced it in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5840, but keeping it undocumented, and we'll likely remove it in the future — but I assume not before we support keyboard customization.
author: dwelle

body:
@dwelle It would be better to update the changelog always in the PR or at least add a pr link in the change log and we can review it before releasing the version.
The reason is, we might miss out when releasing the version as its harder to track what changes need to be added to the changelog once commits are merged, also the changelog is auto-generated for the commits which are not present under the API release hence this could be missed out assuming it's not part of the API.

>  Don't bind to container when using text tool as no container is selected. Checked few other tools like Miro, whimsical, Eraser work similar way.

Not so sure about this one. I'd also do that in a separate PR if anything, as it's a (breaking) feature unrelated to this fix.

But we shouldn't remove the double-click-on-center-to-bind behavior, which seems to now be gone as well (non-filled element).
author: dwelle

body:
> > Don't bind to container when using text tool as no container is selected. Checked few other tools like Miro, whimsical, Eraser work similar way.
> 
> Not so sure about this one. I'd also do that in a separate PR if anything, as it's a (breaking) feature unrelated to this fix.

I am ok with creating a separate PR but the reason I think we should remove is it will have ambiguity which I am fixing in this PR since we don't know which container will be selected hence I find it more intuitive not to bind when using text tool. If we agree I will push that change first and then merge this one.  created a separate issue for the same https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5884
> 
> But we shouldn't remove the double-click-on-center-to-bind behavior, which seems to now be gone as well (non-filled element).

Good catch, fixed it and added a spec for the same

author: vercel

body:
> However, if we still want to pursue a smarter default radius that decreases as the sizes of rectangular elements (rectangles and diamonds) increase, I'll work on it on the previous PR.

I say we do, because the idea of tools like Excalidraw is so that you *don't* have to fiddle with the settings, and just draw things.

Let's say you want to create a quick grid diagram like this:

(below is the previous PR)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204355627-ba92db16-af53-4c53-81ea-985586b11446.png)

In the current PR, you get this:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/204355716-f88c747b-ed8b-4d0f-b1c2-ae14ef3ebf16.png)

You'd have to manually tinker with the radia of each shape so it looks similar. And redo the whole thing if you resize.
author: dwelle

body: Btw, if we keep the slider, my idea was not to have 2 settings: "smart" and "fixed". But make it always "smart", no matter the setting.

body: Hey @3ba2ii! Thanks, but the bug comes form an internal component in Excalidraw Plus (now fixed), not from the ToolButton. The checks you're fixing are actually redundant, as all the types for those are either `undefined | boolean | <truthyValue>`, so it won't ever render the falsy values. Exception being the `props.icon` which is `ReactNode` and could be passed e.g. a `0`, but in practice it won't.

body: Tests for the ColorPicker would be helpful - however I am afraid I won't have the bandwidth to create those. In terms of this specific issue with Electron browser, I don't think a test would have picked this up.
author: zsviczian

body:
> A six-digit hex color string e.g. "ba5f36" was recognized as a valid color by isValidColor even though it did not have a leading #.

Cannot reproduce on latest desktop Obsidian 1.0.3 (Windows), Electron version `18.3.5` (`process.versions.electron`).

`isValidColor('ba5f36')` returns `false` for me. Electron is just Chromium. Would be major for Blink if setting `style.color` regressed in such a major way.
author: dwelle

body: @dwelle I am aware that Electron is just chromium. I am also on `18.3.5`. I did not want to complicate the description with details... This issue only happens if Excalidraw is open in an Obsidian popout window not in the main workspace.

body:
@ad1992 , @dwelle ,
I might be misunderstanding something here... but it seems that with this change, the custom component a.k.a. custom footer no longer displays in the mobile menu. I don't understand the logic. If a custom component/action is implemented, like, for example, element comments in the example code, then you'd want to make that available in mobile view as well.
author: zsviczian

body:
> @ad1992 , @dwelle , I might be misunderstanding something here... but it seems that with this change, the custom component a.k.a. custom footer no longer displays in the mobile menu. I don't understand the logic. If a custom component/action is implemented, like, for example, element comments in the example code, then you'd want to make that available in mobile view as well.

@zsviczian the footer will go into Hamburger menu in menu, I will take care of this in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6034

body: @ad1992 Excellent, then I will wait with using the customer footer. Thanks!

author: vercel

body:
Checking the code, I realized we may not able to do this.

The core of the problem is we don't want to copy the text when copying to clipboard. We need to keep the element's JSON so that when pasting in-app, we paste the element itself, not its text. So it wouldn't work if we wanted to keep copying text if one of the elements in the selected elements was text.

One way to work around this would be to copy as `{ "text/plain": "<element text>", "application/json: "<element json>" }`, and when pasting on canvas (not inside text editor), we'd use the json.

There are several problems with this:

1. I believe there's no cross-browser support to _programmatically_ copy multiple MIME types into the clipboard (namely Firefox & Safari). While it should work for the `copy` event, if we were to reintroduce `copy` action into the contextMenu and elsewhere, it wouldn't work for that.
2. It'd break pasting between Excalidraw editors out there that wouldn't know how to parse this.
3. Would be inconsistent in that sometimes the system clipboard would contain JSON, something text.
4. worse developer experience (we often copy JSON to paste into text editors to debug. Now it would always paste the text when text element was copied). This is arguably less important factor as it doesn't affect users.

The reason why we can't just parse on `paste` event, and change the text being pasted to the parsed element text, is that browsers don't allow to update the `event.cliboardData` (which is something I only remembered when debugging this PR).

So another solution would be to reimplement the textarea editor pasting behavior, which is something we recently did to solve another bug, but reverted because it broke too many thing, and I would not recommend doing this. But it can be revisited once we start using non-textarea text editors, such as [Lexical](https://github.com/facebook/lexical).

---

The programmatic copying, and breaking older editors are the main blockers here, and we don't come up with a solution other than the two above, we'll need to put this on hold.

body: @valgussev Do you plan this PR to permit opening (possibly builtin) context menus in custom locations?  Eg in #2993 I let subtype toggle buttons provide a context menu (I use this for selection of LaTeX versus AsciiMath input).
author: DanielJGeiger

body: @dwelle @ad1992 Could #6008 join #5887?
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
> @valgussev Do you plan this PR to permit opening (possibly builtin) context menus in custom locations? Eg in #2993 I let subtype toggle buttons provide a context menu (I use this for selection of LaTeX versus AsciiMath input).

Here I'm interested in having more control over the contextMenu event and is open for discussions. More specifically I want to render different action options in the contextMenu (with #5983 it should become possible) as well as having better control over styling. So far no plans for rendering contextMenu in custom locations.
author: valgussev

body:
> Here I'm interested in having more control over the contextMenu event and is open for discussions. More specifically I want to render different action options in the contextMenu (with #5983 it should become possible) as well as having better control over styling. So far no plans for rendering contextMenu in custom locations.

Do you mean changing which actions get included in the context menu?  In #2993, I provide some means for that, including custom actions as well.  However, I haven't exposed `executeAction` to `@excalidraw/excalidraw` like you do in #5983.
author: DanielJGeiger

body:
> Do you mean changing which actions get included in the context menu?

Correct, I want a different set of built-in actions in my apps context menu, both this change and #5983 make it possible.

author: vercel

body:
Hello! 

I still don't understand what the original issue was about, or what this PR is trying to fix.

If it's to make holding spacebar pan the canvas without dragging the mouse (i.e. clicking down on canvas and moving your mouse while you still hold the left mouse button), then we don't want to do that because that would go against the standard behavior. (Note that this PR is not doing that.)
author: dwelle

body: @dwelle The original issue as I understood it was that when a user would try to grab something by holding down the spacebar and then drag that item by moving the mouse with the spacebar down, the grab icon would show but nothing would move. So by capturing the last mouse positions when the key spacebar is pushed down...we act as if it is a pointer down event and allow the items selected to be dragged. This does work and does fix the issue. Before, the grabbing icon would show and nothing would move. I attempted to fix this as @TinQk identified this in [issue #6007](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/6007)
author: Jeffsingh

body: Hmmm I'd have to agree with the solution that @dwelle made earlier in #6015. The issue seemed to focus on moving the canvas more than moving individual items. So just switching the mouse icon makes it more clear!
author: mickremedi

body: Although I do like the idea of the hotkey click-and-drag elements functionality. It could speed up the flow of someone drawing shapes and wanting to move them quickly while not leaving whichever shape tool they're currently at. It could be worth bringing up as a new binding?
author: mickremedi

body: @mickremedi how would the hotkey binding work?
author: Jeffsingh

body: @Jeffsingh Say you're currently creating a bunch of circles for a diagram, but while you're drawing, you realize you need to move one of the drawings out of the way. Currently, you would have to press a key to switch to selection mode, move the drawing, and press a key to switch back to whatever tool you're using. A possible approach is that you would hold a modifier key (e.g. CMD/CTRL) and you temporarily switch back to selection mode, where you do the quick move, and when you let go of the key, you go back to whatever tool you were using before.
author: mickremedi

body:
I figured out a way to store my files (images included) in a restic repository, while using excalidraw as the "front end".

It is still a work in progress. The video below demonstrates the gap (literally) that needs to be crossed.

**note that this is me interacting with a [restic repository I created](https://github.com/public-rant/public-rant/blob/master/create.snapshots.mjs) which has snapshots containing [armoured public/private keys](https://github.com/public-rant/public-rant/blob/master/generate.mjs)*

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/82001325/220261168-fb6a7d28-b293-4e96-86d6-f5a58f4fcc8f.MOV

Essentially, I can freeze my [restic snapshots in my bookmarks](https://github.com/public-rant/public-rant/blob/master/render.bookmarks.mjs), and those bookmarks are links to [excalidraw drawings](https://github.com/public-rant/public-rant/blob/master/bookmarks.html).

I can update the content of the snapshot by editing the excalidraw drawing. And by updating my bookmarks, the changes make their way back into my restic repository because of services I run with docker (nginx/restic/excalidraw).

I plan to continue development using the [MUI data tables library](https://codesandbox.io/examples/package/mui-datatables). There is a [small set of features](https://github.com/public-rant/public-rant/blob/master/render.bookmarks.mjs#L21-L25) that would need to be implemented to move from a WIP to a MVP.
